title: Why Love Matters
body:
Love matters because it shapes the deepest human commitments: partnership, family, friendship, loyalty, and grief. Much of what gives life meaning is not achievement but attachment. People build their lives around those bonds, and they feel their absence when they break.

Love also matters socially. Families, communities, and societies depend on care that is sustained over time, often without immediate reward. That is what makes love more than a private feeling. It is a social force.

Finally, love matters because it teaches vulnerability. To love is to accept that another person can change your life, disappoint you, or leave. That risk is part of why love is valuable at all.
keyPoints: Love shapes the most important human relationships
It is a major source of meaning and resilience
Caring bonds support families and communities
Love and grief are closely linked
Healthy love requires vulnerability and trust
Understanding love helps people build better relationships
furtherReading: 'The Road Less Traveled' by M. Scott Peck
'How to Be an Adult in Relationships' by David Richo
